concerns.text: T025 (fit_models.py) depends on the stratified sample generated by T024 (sample_data.py), yet T025 is marked [P] (parallel-safe). T024 must complete before T025 can execute; they cannot run in parallel.

T026 (cross_val.py) implements spatial cross-validation which requires the fitted models from T025. T026 is marked [P], implying it can run in parallel with T025, but it is a strict consumer of T025's output.

T027 (perm_test.py) performs a permutation test to validate model results, requiring the fitted models from T025. Marking T027 as [P] suggests it can run in parallel with T025, violating the producer-consumer dependency.

T028 (report_metrics.py) aggregates results from T025 (models), T026 (CV metrics), and T027 (permutation p-values). It is marked [P] but strictly depends on the completion of all three preceding tasks.

T029 (sensitivity analysis on GWR bandwidth) requires the GWR model fitting logic from T025 to sweep parameters. It is marked [P] but cannot execute until T025 is implemented and the model interface is stable.
T030 (sensitivity analysis on albedo) requires the modeling pipeline from T025 to re-run with modified inputs. It is marked [P] but is a consumer of the T025 artifact.

T013 (align_rasters.py) consumes the OSM data from T011 and thermal data from T012. T013 is NOT marked [P], which is correct, but T011 and T012 are marked [P]. While T011/T012 can run in parallel, T013 must strictly follow them. The current grouping implies T013 might be parallel with T011/T012 if not carefully sequenced, though the phase structure helps. However, the lack of explicit dependency notation in the task list for T013 is a semantic risk.

T020 (fallback to OLS) depends on the spatial autocorrelation results from T018 (Moran's I). T020 is not marked [P], which is correct, but the dependency chain T018 -> T020 must be explicit. T018 is marked [P], which is fine as it produces data for T020.
